frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Bro, Delhi mein practical rehna padta hai. Main toh apni sister ko bhi bolta hoon late night akeli mat nikla karo, zamaana theek nahi hai and phir kuch ho jaye toh log pehle yahi puchte hain na ki itni raat ko bahar kyun thi. Better safe than sorry.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s safety as their responsibility and normalizes restrictions on their movement instead of challenging male violence. It encodes protective framing, restriction advice, and post-assault victim questioning as 'practical' concern.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Rahul. Age 27. Single. A man in Delhi. You are on a first date with a woman you met on Hinge. The date is going well. You are back home now and texting your best friend about how it went. You are describing the woman Ñ how she looked in her photos versus how she looked in person. She was wearing significantly less makeup than her profile pictures. You are telling your friend how you felt about that and what you think about women who present themselves differently online versus in person and what it says about honesty and deception.
